title: Interesting parallels
wikitext:
[]: Interesting parallels...             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Tue Oct 25, 2005 2:03 am Has anybody noticed that the new trend in weird psuedo-religeous super robo anime seems to have some striking similarities to the classic super robos from days gone by? Examples... Eva/Mazinger Z: Both fought monsters who came from an ancient civilization who'd been buried for millenia. (Machine Beasts from the Mikenese empire/Angels from White Moon) Both have the pilot get into the cockpit in a seperate component that then plugs into the mecha. (Hoverpiloter/Entry Plug) Both involve a creepy, inhuman-looking evil scientist villain with delusions of grandure. (Dr. Jigoku/Kiel) Both have the hero's love interest(s) & his goofy friend get their own mecha. (Aphrodite A, Minerva X & Bossborot/00, 02 & 03) Both feature an androgynous, possibly homosexual villain character. (Ashura/Kaworu) Both the hero super robos are vaguely demonic in design. (Mazinger was designed after a demon character in an earlier Go Nagai manga with a human face sticking out of his forehead & his name comes from Majin which apparently is a kind of evil spirit/01 is allegedly designed after a baron of Hell) Brave Raideen/RahXephon: I've never actually seen this, but I do know of a lot of similarities. Both the hero robos have predominately blue & yellow colour schemes, similar facial features & posess bird-like characteristics. Both the robos were created by the mythological Mu empire. Interestingly, in Raideen, Mu is show to be similar to Egypt, whereas RahXephon shows the Mulians as having a Mayan-esque design sense, but the name of the show's title mecha is an allusion to the Egyptian god, Rah. Both the main characters are at least part Mulian. (Akira Hibiki/Ayato Kamina) Both Raideen & RahXephon are said to be something other than true robots. RahXephon is a Doremi, a giant robo-thing made from a clay-like substance & animated by psychic energy & Raideen, while made from metal like a robot is apparently also animated by some kind of mystic force. Raideen was the first super robo show to have a prettyboy villain (Prince Sharkin) & being a 21st century anime, RahXephon has plenty of those. Getta Robo/Aquarion: Ok, this has got to be the most obvious one. Aquarion is composed of 3 different fighter jets that can combine in 3 different ways to form 3 different super robos. Sound familiar? Get! Get! Getta! Get-ta-ripoff! My prediction for the next show after Aquarion's over: A 5-piece combiner, ala Combattler V who fights creepy angel things.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Oct 25, 2005 6:01 am Fafner, also. Can't forget Fafner. :o

[]:             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Tue Oct 25, 2005 2:40 pm sachiel wrote: Fafner, also. Can't forget Fafner. :o

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Oct 25, 2005 8:54 pm Most people generally compare RahXephon and Evangelion together. And it's probably because the shows that came before influenced the creator of the newer show one way or another...
